DQFSfLbEs1h.caption:
12 likes, 0 comments - ubclassa on October 21, 2025: "Did you know that as an iSchool student at UBC, you are automatically a member of LASSA? Here is a breakdown of what LASSA is, what that means for you, and some resources that we provide to students.

✏️What is LASSA?
- LASSA is the Library and Archival Studies Student Association
- Every student in UBC’s iSchool is automatically a member of LASSA. This includes MLIS, MAS, Dual, MaCL, and PhD students.
- We are run by students, for students. 

✏️What can LASSA do for you?
- Advocate for you as a student and bring your concerns to the iSchool office and faculty members.
- Represent the iSchool students for AMS, GSS, and iSchool administration.
- Put on fun events for iSchool students.
- Help promote and sponsor other iSchool or information studies-related events and announcements.

✏️LASSA Resources 
All of these resources are available on our website (link in bio).
- A list of clubs and organizations relevant to iSchool students
- PDF of our constitution and bylaws 
- Meeting minutes
- Descriptions of our executive positions 
- And more!

You can contact LASSA, view our meeting minutes on our website, or even request to join a meeting at any time. Our email is lassa.ubc@gmail.com, and our DM is open. 📖".

Applied to youCode 2026 but not sure how to get started? Want to feel more confident going into the hackathon?

Join our workshop series designed to help you build, design, and collaborate! 

🎨 Intro to Figma, UX & UI w/ @ubcuxhub

Open to hackers who applied to youCode 2026 only.
Spots are limited — RSVP through the link in our bio 🌟".

DUo7BJLkQ-V.caption:
82 likes, 3 comments - ubcpmc on February 11, 2026: "Product Sprint is back 💥 💜

Product Sprint is our yearly flagship product management competition; a two-week, hands-on challenge where you tackle a real product problem and move through the 4 core phases of product development, just like PM teams do in industry:

🔍 Discovery
🧠 Product Planning
🎨 Prototyping
🎤 Presentation

Along the way, you’ll get:
🤝 mentorship from industry professionals (mentor list coming soon!)
🛠️ workshops aligned with each stage
📁 a portfolio-ready case study (research + strategy + design + execution)
✨ new this year: learn low/no-code AI prototyping tools to build functional prototypes (no technical background needed)

🗓️ Competition dates: March 1 – March 15, 2026
Registration closes: February 21 at 11:59 PM

Team setup:
- Everyone registers individually
- Teams of 4
- Teams are finalized after acceptance
- No team? No stress - we’ll match you into one

Pricing:
🎟️ FREE for PMC Members
💳 $6.20 for Non-members

🔗 Register via the link in bio - spots are limited 🚀

#ubcpmc #productsprint #ubc #productmanagement #ubcevents".

DV-T7IwCThU.caption:
ubcpmc on March 16, 2026: "🎉 Join us for our Presidential & Treasurer Election and final pmCommunity social of the school year! 🎉

Join us next week for a pmCommunity gathering where we’ll be electing the next PMC President(s) and Treasurer for the 2026–2027 year, and celebrating the end of an amazing year with the PMC community 💜

This is not just an election. It’s also your chance to meet the team, connect with other members, and get a feel for what being part of PMC is all about! Whether you’re looking to get more involved or just want to hang out, we’d love to see you there ✨

What to expect:
🎤 Candidate pitches Hear each candidate share their vision for PMC
🗳️ Election voting Have your say in next year’s team
💜 Meet the PMC team + year-end social Connect with execs and members over snacks and conversation
📣 2026/27 hiring timeline Learn what’s coming next and how to stand out ahead of exec hiring

Details
📅 Monday, March 23⏰ 6:30 to 7:30 PM📍 Henry Angus Room 237
Come for the election, stay for the people, and help shape the future of PMC 🙌
‼️ Note: Only current UBC Students only.".

DWAmrrdDTHb.caption:
ubcnus on March 17, 2026: "Join us for a relaxed and welcoming conversation between faculty and undergraduate students 🫶

📅 Monday, March 30
⏰ 12:00 to 1:00 PM
📍 Gateway 1003 (same as 350/353)

Hosted by Dr. Nassim Adhami, with guest faculty members Dr. Emily Jenkins and Dr. Fuchsia Howard, this event is part of an ongoing series focused on strengthening connections within our nursing and healthcare community 🤝

Dr. Jenkins is a Professor and Canada Research Chair whose work explores socioecological approaches to mental health and substance use.
Dr. Howard’s research focuses on the healthcare needs of survivors of critical illness and cancer, with an emphasis on improving patient and family centred care.
Dr. Adhami studies transitions of care for individuals living with chronic disease, with interests in health equity, rural healthcare, and person centred care, and she also practices in adult outpatient cardiology.

During this lunch hour session, students are invited to drop in, bring their own meal or coffee ☕, and engage in open and supportive conversation with experienced nurses.

Past sessions have created space to talk about managing anxiety during clinical placements, coping with grief and patient loss, and exploring different career paths after graduation.

Come by to connect, ask questions, and share experiences in a supportive environment. Snacks will be provided 🍪".

8 likes, 0 comments - ubclss on March 12, 2026: "HELLO ALL 1Ls!!! Join us for an evening of great food, refreshments, and the chance to sing your heart out in support of a meaningful cause.

DVt1452ksbw.caption:
10 likes, 0 comments - ubclss on March 10, 2026: "Join us on March 27 for our annual TRIKE RACE!!!

Trike Race is a historic event at Allard Law that has been put on since 1972. Teams of students, faculty, staff, and lawyers will race on trikes while everyone else cheers them on, throws water balloons at them, and sprays them with water guns! After the race everyone will be invited to the Lew Forum for drinks, food, and the awarding of trophies.

There will be 2 awards: One prize for the winning team and another for the team with the best spirit.

This year the theme is F1 and we encourage everyone to wear costumes. The teams have freedom to interpret the theme in their team name and attire however they like.

The race will start at 4pm and will be followed by a celebratory Friday Social in the Lew Forum!".

DBzSoCuT0Bu.caption:
rootsontheroof on October 31, 2024: "FARM TO PLATE MARKETPLACE/HUB!

🌾 WHAT IS THIS? 

F2PM is a tool for communities to use to empower themselves to collectively support local food producers. Through the platform’s transparency, communities can better understand and address the challenges of our food system and tackle food insecurity. F2PM encourages eating locally and seasonally, promotes community development, and facilitates communal problem solving. Food is a powerful connector that can bring people together to better understand and support one another.

🌿 For any questions, reach out to ubcf2pmhub@gmail.com

🌾 F2MP VS FOOD BANK AS A LONG-TERM SLN

A food bank, on the other hand, requires constant funding from outside the community and as a long term model, is unsustainable as the community must constantly fight for the right to access.

🌾 HOW DOES THIS WORK

Using a pay-what-you-can model, users may:
pay the cost, pay it forward, or use a subsidy.
Payments are by cash or etransfer

🌾 STEPS:
1. Food vendors upload their available items every week for customers to browse and order. 

2. Community members can order on their own, or call a volunteer to help them place their order.

3.Pay what you can after signing in at farmtoplatemarketplace.com

🌿LINK IN BIO!

4. Choose a pickup location (UBC Forestry if you’re on campus!)

#rootsontheroof".
